notes_this_run: Mode 2 (Human Notes) processed: HNOTE-OPERATOR-EXP-254-9-STATION-COVERAGE-001 consumed by writing EXP-260. New addendum to §4.2.7 (the eclipse-geometry trilemma) gives the quantitative station-by-station kill: of the dome's 9 published eclipse stations, 0 lie within the 42-km dome-geometry penumbra radius and 0 within the 85-km band (closest Ebro at 444 km / 10.6x R_p; furthest Canary Islands at 4,084 km / 97x R_p). Constant-r orbital reading: max 1 station can be in the band at all (closest pair Coimbra-San Pablo dr=150 km > 85-km band). Inflated R_p needed for full coverage = 4,084 km, equivalent to Moon altitude ~246,000 km (97x dome's 2,534). Pre-empts time-distribution rebuttal: NN-tour 16,439 km > 15,660 km max arc at 87 km/min. New SVG eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg embedded in EXP-260 new_files. Blocked on EXP-254 integration. Hnote pending: 0.

Mode 1 (Section Expansion) @ 2026-04-26T23:06:19.900Z. DISPATCHER TRACE: Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: clone=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK. Mode 1 — Expansion queue: 1 actionable pending (EXP-262). FIRED. (Modes 1b–4 not evaluated this run; Mode 1 has priority and produces work.) WORK PERFORMED: Wrote monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-262-refraction-preemption.json (~165 word_count_delta, 1350 chars HTML). Updated monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json: EXP-262 status=complete, completed_at=2026-04-26T23:06:19.900Z, output_file=monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-262-refraction-preemption.json. Both files JSON-validated. EXP-262 CONTENT SUMMARY (addresses curmudgeon EXP-254 hole H1, severity moderate): New

Robustness under refraction

subsection inserted between §4.2.7's Kernel-of-truth and Counterargument subsections. Pre-empts dome's published 'observed (5,733 km) vs. physical (<4,200 km)' Sun-altitude distinction and the implied aetheric-refraction defensive opening. Math-verified: under proportional physical-altitude scaling (h_Sun=4,200 / h_Moon=1,856), totality diameter shrinks from ~1.32 km to ~0.97 km — sharpens, does not soften, the geometric mismatch. Sensitivity analysis: even asymmetric scaling (h_Sun=4,200 / h_Moon=2,534 unchanged) only widens totality to ~1.85 km — still ~1/160 of the observed 294-km path. Demand on dome: any refractive-shadow rescue must (a) be non-derivable from standard occultation, AND (b) reproduce both observed path width AND station-by-station coverage. None published in formula_runtime.json. DISCREPANCY NOTED AND RESOLVED: EXP-262 tracker notes referenced ~0.58 km diameter under physical altitudes — does not reproduce in independent re-derivation under any reasonable refractive scaling. Curmudgeon c1 of EXP-254 cited ~0.9 km diameter — agrees with my independent re-derivation (0.964 km, cited as ~0.97 km). Used the math-verified ~0.97 km figure in the prose; documented the discrepancy in EXP-262's math_verification.rounding_note for traceability. DECIDER ROUTING: Two integration paths offered (preferred: pre-integration fold into EXP-254's still-pending replacement_html before §4.2.7 lands in sections.json; fallback: post-integration substring patch in part4.html). Curmudgeon's c1 explicitly recommended pre-integration fold (suggested_sequencing step 1 in EXP-254-proposal.json). Re-queue §4.2.7 for curmudgeon spot-check after integration (curmudgeon-cycle 2). No additional EXPs created this run for H2-H7; expected as separate items. USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side too'): Strongest defender pushback against EXP-262's paragraph: 'A genuine n(r) ray-tracing solution might bend light from a 4,200-km-high Sun in such a way that the apparent shadow on the disc covers a 294-km path despite local geometry suggesting otherwise.' This is fair. The paragraph addresses it head-on by demanding the dome publish such a model, and by setting an explicit falsifiable bar (path width AND station-by-station coverage). The paragraph deliberately does not pre-emptively rule out the existence of such a mechanism — it sets the bar and points out it has not been published. Captured in EXP-262's other_side_of_the_argument field. NOT DONE (intentional): Did NOT modify EXP-254's replacement_html directly — the decider chooses pre-integration fold vs. post-integration patch. Did NOT touch wins.json, sections.json, predictions.json, or any data file. Did NOT push or commit. Workspace-sync universal-pusher will rescue EXP-262 + tracker on its next 4h run. Did NOT process Mode 1b/2/2b/3/4 — Mode 1 had priority and consumed this run. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for analyst: 0 actionable expansions pending. Mode 2 has 1 human note (HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-001-TESLA-PREDICTION-VS-MEASUREMENT-001). Mode 2b has 70 pending in clone (workspace state may differ). Suggest Mode 2 first, then resume Mode 2b three-phase drain. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for decider: New expansion EXP-262 ready for integration. Curmudgeon-recommended sequencing is pre-integration fold into EXP-254 (single sections.json edit, not two). After fold + EXP-254 integration, run build + tests. Re-queue §4.2.7 for curmudgeon c2. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for curmudgeon: After §4.2.7 + refraction-preemption integration, spot-check that the math figure (~0.97 km vs. c1's ~0.9 km) reproduces and that the asymmetric-scaling sensitivity (~1.85 km) is correctly reported. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE for tinker: No pipeline-health concerns surfaced this run. EXP-262 followed the JSON.stringify build pattern (per analyst.md mandate); validation passed first try.

PHASE 3 — SINGLETON SUBSTANTIVE (ATT-2026-04-25T05:27:36Z, WIN-010 c10):
  Reason flagged ISS-1523 ("multiplicative-error claim needs proof or softening — persisting c6–c9 hole") as the priority concern. Verification:
  (a) ISS-1523 status: FIXED via EXP-258, integrated 2026-04-26T13:27:55.791Z (today).
  (b) WIN-010 detail_verdict_text (9477c) no longer contains "approximately common multiplicative error" (the unargued claim).
  (c) Now contains "sub-dominant compared to the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint" + a pre-registered sensitivity check with α ∈ {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} for the post-2026-08-12 analysis, including an explicit "we will say so" falsification commit if the ranking flips.
  This fix is STRONGER than the issue's minimum-acceptable softening because it (i) flags the methodological limit explicitly, (ii) commits α range in advance (preventing post-hoc tuning), (iii) makes the failure mode public. The decider's EXP-258 work is solid.
  ISS-1522 (Chapman–Sq em-dash hyphenation) and ISS-1524 (K&C page range / DOI footnote) remain OPEN but are cosmetic/low-stakes — outside the scope of this attention item's primary flagged concern.

USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ("Think about the other side too"): Considered the strongest defender objection — that {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} is too narrow if true Sq-conductivity nonlinearity sits far from α=1 (e.g., α=0.5 or 1.5). Accepted as a known limit and noted that the WIN itself acknowledges this ("the magnitude of any nonlinear-coverage correction is not derived in this WIN"). The pre-registration constraint matters more than range width — selecting α to fit results is precluded regardless of bracket width. Counter-argument considered and accepted; resolution stands.
